accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Ecliptic of J2000.0
accuracyRecords.limitation: The current fallback bundle has no Horizons window at this timestamp; Helios propagates a bounded two-body osculating preview from the source vector.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Earth mean ecliptic at J2000
accuracyRecords.limitation: JPL fitted mean elements describe general orbit shape and orientation. Precession metadata is not propagated, so the rendered date position is representative rather than an ephemeris.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Heliocentric Earth mean ecliptic and equinox of J2000
accuracyRecords.limitation: A published JPL SBDB sample element set is propagated with a two-body solver for visual preview only; use Horizons for accurate ephemerides.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Heliocentric Earth mean ecliptic and equinox of J2000
accuracyRecords.limitation: An accepted frozen six-element preview is retained to preserve the existing body scope. It was not freshly re-fetched from SBDB in this build environment and must not be presented as an accurate or current ephemeris.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Heliocentric Earth mean ecliptic and equinox of J2000
accuracyRecords.limitation: A frozen two-body element set is propagated for visual preview. Planetary perturbations and comet non-gravitational acceleration are not integrated; Horizons is required for date-accurate positions.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Pluto equatorial plane
accuracyRecords.limitation: Pluto–Charon is shown around a shared visual barycentre using sourced size, mean separation and a source-backed near-zero inclination to Pluto's equator. Node/periapsis orientation remains representative.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Representative parent-equatorial visual plane; source-backed pole unresolved
accuracyRecords.limitation: Mean orbit scale and period are representative; a common source-backed J2000 pole solution was not available in the accepted source set.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Haumea equatorial plane
accuracyRecords.limitation: Published mean system scale and the 2° inclination to Haumea's equator are used. Missing node/periapsis orientation remains representative.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Haumea equatorial plane
accuracyRecords.limitation: The non-circular mean orbit and 13° inclination to Haumea's equator are used without inventing missing node/periapsis angles.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Representative parent-equatorial visual plane; source-backed pole unresolved
accuracyRecords.limitation: Only a restrained representative separation/period context is rendered. No surface map or precise angular orbit is claimed.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Representative parent-equatorial visual plane; source-backed pole unresolved
accuracyRecords.limitation: Mean separation, period and eccentricity are representative. Unresolved orientation remains explicit rather than randomized.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Representative parent-equatorial visual plane; source-backed pole unresolved
accuracyRecords.limitation: A representative eccentric orbit is shown; pole and longitude are not asserted.

accuracyRecords.referencePlane: Representative parent-equatorial visual plane; source-backed pole unresolved
accuracyRecords.limitation: Mean orbit scale and period are representative. The orientation is intentionally not navigation-grade.

regressionEvidence.samplingBug: Uniform mean-anomaly samples used for line geometry created long perihelion and closure chords on highly eccentric rendered orbits.
regressionEvidence.fix: Date positions continue to use mean anomaly and a Kepler solve. Rendered paths use dense eccentric-anomaly samples and are re-sampled by rendered arc length after final scene scaling.
